title: psyllium parviflorum
intro: Psyllium parviflorum, also known as the Indian psyllium, is a plant species that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural laxative and absorbent, due to its high fiber content. The health benefits of Psyllium parviflorum include reducing cholesterol levels, regulating bowel movements, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular herbal remedy for gastrointestinal issues. Psyllium parviflorum can be prepared in various forms, such as capsules, tablets, or powder, and is often consumed as a dietary supplement to promote overall well-being.
